steps.title: Use the surprise signal
steps.content: The passage explains that Merino wool is soft overall, while Navajo-Churro wool is coarser overall and useful for durable rugs. The word yet signals that the table will support a surprising comparison among the mouflon, Merino, and Navajo-Churro fiber measurements.

steps.title: Read the inner fiber data
steps.content: The key values are the minimum inner fiber diameters. Navajo-Churro's minimum inner fiber is 10 micrometers, while the mouflon's is 15 micrometers and the Merino's is 17 micrometers. A smaller diameter means finer, softer fiber at the softest end of the range.

steps.title: Check the claims
steps.content: Choice B wrongly describes Navajo-Churro inner fiber as uniform. Choice C wrongly says domestication increased the Navajo-Churro minimum inner fiber diameter. Choice D overclaims greater softness in both outer and inner fiber. The claim about Navajo-Churro's softest inner fiber being softer than the others matches the table. The answer is A.
